narrative: This is placeholder narrative: On March 6, 2026, the institution identified an attempted outbound wire transfer in the amount of USD 18,450 involving ABC Import Services as originator and Tehran Industrial Supply Co. as beneficiary, with the payment reference indicating settlement for industrial components. The transaction generated a sanctions alert during automated screening due to the beneficiary's location in Iran, a comprehensively sanctioned jurisdiction. Review of the payment message, invoice INV-2026-0112, customer account records, and sanctions screening output confirmed that the beneficiary was located in Iran and that the beneficiary financial institution, Bank Melli Iran, is itself a sanctioned entity. Based on the available information, the institution determined that the transaction could not be lawfully processed due to its involvement with a sanctioned jurisdiction and entity under the Iran Transactions and Sanctions Regulations (ITSR), 31 C.F.R. Part 560. The transaction was therefore rejected and was not processed. Relevant records, including the payment message, invoice, customer account records, and screening results, were retained in connection with the reporting process.
